hero.title: Becoming a CNA in Florida
hero.dek: Learn Florida Board of Nursing requirements, find state-approved programs, and prepare for the state exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Florida | Requirements & Steps
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in Florida. Learn about Level 2 Background checks, Prometric exam challenge routes, and training.
target_queries: how to become a cna in florida

becoming.faq_heading: Common Questions about Getting a CNA in Florida

sections.label: Basic Requirements
sections.body: Florida offers a unique 'Challenger Route' where candidates 18 or older with a high school diploma can take the state exam without completing a formal program, though passing the Level II background check is mandatory regardless of route.

faq.q: Can I challenge the CNA exam in Florida?
faq.a: Yes, Florida allows individuals 18 or older with a high school diploma to challenge the CNA exam without attending a formal CNA program.

hero.title: Florida CNA State Exam Application
hero.dek: Apply to take the Florida CNA competency evaluation.

seo.title: Florida CNA Exam Application & Registry Guide
seo.description: How to apply for the Florida CNA state exam via Prometric and get listed on the Florida Nurse Aide Registry.

sections.label: Application Steps
sections.body: Submit your application to the Board and Prometric, pass the Level II background screening, and schedule your exam.

faq.q: Who administers the Florida CNA exam?
faq.a: The CNA certification exam in Florida is administered by Prometric.

hero.title: Transfer a CNA License to Florida
hero.dek: Florida allows out-of-state CNAs to transfer via Reciprocity (Endorsement) if they hold an active certificate and pass a background check.

seo.title: Transfer CNA License to Florida | Endorsement Steps
seo.description: Learn how to transfer your active out-of-state CNA license to the Florida Nurse Aide Registry via Endorsement.

transfer.faq_heading: Questions about Transferring your CNA to Florida

sections.label: Transfer Requirements
sections.body: You must hold a clear, active out-of-state CNA license, pass the Florida Level II background screening, and submit an Endorsement application. No state exam retake is required if approved.

faq.q: Does Florida accept out of state CNAs?
faq.a: Yes, Florida accepts active CNAs from other states via the Endorsement process, provided you clear their Level II background check with no disqualifying findings.
